summary: WTI crude ended last week up 35% — its biggest weekly gain since 1983 — as the U.S.-Iran war choked Strait of Hormuz traffic, while February payrolls shocked with a 92,000 job loss. The stagflationary mix of a weakening economy and spiking oil-driven inflation leaves the Fed with no clean policy tool, pressuring stocks and crypto into fear while channeling safe-haven flows into gold and whipsawing bonds between growth fears and inflation risk.
